indicator.name: DXY Trade Alert
indicator.description: Detects two trade setups (Attraction and Reversal) on the DXY during the London session. Uses the 23:45 GMT Open Zone as the structural reference level. Signals fire only when confirmed by a candlestick entry pattern within the correct session window.

open_zone.definition: The structural reference zone formed at 23:45 GMT. Primary method: gap between the close of the prior candle and the 23:45 open. Fallback method: if no noticeable gap exists (gap < zone_min_gap threshold, default 30 pts / 0.003 DXY), the body of the 23:45 candle itself (open-to-close) is used as the zone. Monday exception: if the candle immediately before 23:45 is a line candle (body < 10 pts / 0.001 DXY — common on Monday when the weekend gap produces a flat/doji bar), that candle is skipped and the candle before it is used instead.
open_zone.zone_top: If gap >= threshold: math.max(prior_candle_close, japan_open). If no gap: math.max(23:45 open, 23:45 close).
open_zone.zone_bottom: If gap >= threshold: math.min(prior_candle_close, japan_open). If no gap: math.min(23:45 open, 23:45 close).
open_zone.rationale: The 23:45 GMT candle marks the start of the Japan session. The gap between the preceding candle's close and this open defines a structural reference zone that price is expected to interact with during London.
open_zone.pristine_definition: Zone is PRISTINE if all Japan session candle CLOSES remain on the same side of the zone they started on. A close inside the zone is acceptable. Pristine is broken only when a close crosses THROUGH to the opposite side (beyond the far edge). Wicks through the zone are acceptable — only closes matter.
open_zone.pristine_broken: If Japan is bullish (opened above zone), pristine breaks if any candle closes BELOW zone_bottom. If Japan is bearish (opened below zone), pristine breaks if any candle closes ABOVE zone_top.
open_zone.visual: Displayed as a shaded orange box (pristine) or grey box (broken) on the chart.
open_zone.no_trade_condition: If price is ranging tightly around the DXY Open Zone with no clear directional move, or if the zone has been bridged repeatedly during the Japan session, do not trade for that day.

htf_bias.indicator: MTFTI (Multi-Timeframe Trading Indicator — displayed on DXY Open layout in TradingView)
htf_bias.description: MTFTI shows the directional bias on multiple timeframes. The 1H and 4H bias readings are used to filter Reversal trades. This indicator runs alongside DXY Trade Alert on the same chart layout.

weekly_daily_analysis.uptrend: 200 SMA at bottom, 100 SMA in middle, 50 SMA at top
weekly_daily_analysis.downtrend: 200 SMA at top, 100 SMA in middle, 50 SMA at bottom
weekly_daily_analysis.additional: Mark key Supply & Demand zones and Fibonacci levels (0.382 / 0.618) for confluence areas

htf_bias.4h_analysis: Confirm alignment with Daily bias. Assess momentum and MACD divergence. Identify whether price is in continuation or correction.
htf_bias.1h_analysis: Momentum check. Is price returning to the moving average? Assess whether HTF direction supports the trade.

attraction.description: Mean-reversion trade. Price has moved a minimum distance away from the zone during the Japan session and is expected to be attracted back toward the zone during London.
attraction.required_zone_state: BODY-CLEAN — zone must be completely clear of candle bodies throughout the Japan session (with a grace period for the first 3 candles after the 23:45 zone is formed). From the 4th candle onward, if any candle CLOSE is inside the zone, the zone is disqualified for attraction trades. Only the close is checked (not the open or wick). This is stricter than the reversal condition: a close inside the zone (not just beyond the far edge) invalidates the attraction setup.
attraction.htf_bias_requirement: NONE. Attraction trades do NOT require 1H or 4H MTFTI bias alignment. The pristine zone is sufficient context.

direction_logic.long_setup: Japan session is bearish (price opened below zone). Price is 150–500 DXY points below zone_bottom during London. Bullish entry signal fires. Target: zone_TOP (far side of zone). SL: 1:1 with TP distance (same number of DXY points below entry as the entry-to-zone_top distance).
direction_logic.short_setup: Japan session is bullish (price opened above zone). Price is 150–500 DXY points above zone_top during London. Bearish entry signal fires. Target: zone_BOTTOM (far side of zone). SL: 1:1 with TP distance (same number of DXY points above entry as the entry-to-zone_bottom distance). Note: 150 DXY points as measured by TradingView measuring tool = 1500 indicator units (formula multiplies by 10000).

distance_requirement.dxy_point_definition: 1 DXY point = 0.001 DXY price move. DXY uses POINTS, not pips. 150 DXY points = 0.150 DXY price move.
distance_requirement.indicator_units: The indicator formula is dist = (price - zone_edge) × 10000. Therefore: 1 TradingView point (measuring tool) = 0.001 DXY = 10 indicator units. 150 TradingView points = 0.150 DXY = 1500 indicator units. The minimum_pts input is set to 1500 to enforce the 150 DXY point floor.
distance_requirement.minimum_note: Minimum 150 DXY points (0.150 DXY / 1500 indicator pts) required between entry price and zone near edge. This matches what the TradingView measuring tool displays as '150 points'.
distance_requirement.preferred_condition: Slow, gradual movement away from the zone without strong momentum. Strong momentum away from the zone is a warning sign — reassess.

stop_loss.rule: 1:1 RR — SL distance equals TP distance.
stop_loss.long_sl: Entry price minus the distance from entry to zone_top.
stop_loss.short_sl: Entry price plus the distance from entry to zone_bottom.

attraction.momentum_check: Verify Japan session momentum was not strong. If there is strong directional momentum in the Japan session, consider skipping the attraction trade.

reversal.description: Momentum-continuation trade off a broken zone. The Open Zone has been broken (pristine lost), turning the zone into a temporary support or resistance level. Three confirmations are required before the entry signal: an impulsive bounce candle, AlgoPro Market Matrix divergence, and an entry candle pattern.
reversal.required_zone_state: BROKEN — zone pristine status has been lost (a Japan session candle closed beyond the far edge of the zone).
reversal.htf_bias_requirement: REQUIRED (manual check — cannot be automated). Confirm MTFTI 1H AND 4H bias both align with the trade direction before acting on any reversal alert. MTFTI outputs a table only (no plottable series), so this check cannot be enforced in the indicator code. The indicator enforces two other hard conditions: (1) impulsive bounce candle, (2) AlgoPro Market Matrix divergence.

impulsive_bounce_filter.description: A candle whose body confirms a genuine bounce away from the broken zone. Must appear within 5 bars before the entry signal candle.
conditions: Body size >= 2x the body of the nearest previous non-doji candle.
Body size >= 45 DXY points (450 indicator pts / 0.045 DXY price move).
Direction matches the trade direction (bullish body for reversal long, bearish body for reversal short).
Doji definition: body < 5 indicator pts (0.0005 DXY). Doji candles are skipped when finding the previous candle for the 2x comparison.
impulsive_bounce_filter.rationale: Filters out low-conviction bounces and sideways chop around the zone. Only an impulsive move — one that clearly dwarfs the preceding candle — counts as confirmation that price is rejecting the zone level.

algopro_divergence_filter.description: AlgoPro Market Matrix indicator must show divergence matching the trade direction.
conditions: Bullish divergence (AlgoPro output > 0) required for REVERSAL LONG.
Bearish divergence (AlgoPro output < 0) required for REVERSAL SHORT.
algopro_divergence_filter.setup: Add AlgoPro Market Matrix to the chart, then connect its divergence output to the 'AlgoPro Market Matrix — Divergence Signal' source input on the DXY Trade Alert indicator.

setup_sequence: 1. Zone pristine is broken during Japan session (candle closes on the opposing side of the zone).
2. During London session (06:45–19:30 GMT), an impulsive bounce candle fires: body >= 2x nearest prior non-doji body AND >= 45 DXY points (0.045 DXY). Direction must match trade direction.
3. AlgoPro Market Matrix divergence confirms the same direction as the impulsive candle.
4. MANUAL CHECK: Confirm MTFTI 1H AND 4H bias both align with the trade direction (table-only indicator — cannot be automated).
5. Within 5 bars of the impulsive candle, one of the three entry signals fires: Engulfing, Pin Bar, or 3-Bar Reversal.
6. The broken zone acts as the structural reference for Stop Loss placement (behind zone_top for longs, zone_bottom for shorts).
7. Minimum TP: 150 DXY points (1:1 with SL).

direction_logic.long_setup: Zone broken. Bullish impulsive candle + AlgoPro bullish divergence (indicator). Manual check: MTFTI 1H and 4H bullish. Then bullish entry signal. Zone acts as support reference. SL: recent swing low.
direction_logic.short_setup: Zone broken. Bearish impulsive candle + AlgoPro bearish divergence (indicator). Manual check: MTFTI 1H and 4H bearish. Then bearish entry signal. Zone acts as resistance reference. SL: recent swing high.

reversal.no_distance_requirement: There is NO minimum or maximum distance requirement from the zone for reversal trades. The broken zone is simply a structural reference, not a distance gate.
reversal.note_on_sideways: If price action is sideways around the zone with no impulsive move early in the London session, the indicator will not fire — the impulsive bounce filter will suppress the signal.

entry_signals.description: At least one of these three candlestick patterns must form at the entry location during the session window for any alert to trigger. No signal = no trade.
entry_signals.minimum_trade_size_note: All trades (attraction and reversal) require a minimum of 150 DXY points (1500 pts) between entry and the zone. This ensures sufficient room for a 1:1 risk-reward setup.

engulfing_candle.bull: Current bar bullish, prior bar bearish. Current close > prior open, current open < prior close. Current body >= 80% of prior body.
engulfing_candle.bear: Current bar bearish, prior bar bullish. Current close < prior open, current open > prior close. Current body >= 80% of prior body.
engulfing_candle.note: Avoid excessively large engulfing candles — they reduce the quality of the entry.

pin_bar.bull: Lower wick >= body × wick_multiplier (default 2.0). Upper wick <= body × 1.5. Long lower wick preferred.
pin_bar.bear: Upper wick >= body × wick_multiplier (default 2.0). Lower wick <= body × 1.5.

3_bar_reversal.bull: Bar -2 is bearish. Bar -1 is indecision (body <= 50% of bar -2 body). Current bar is bullish and closes above bar -2 open.
3_bar_reversal.bear: Bar -2 is bullish. Bar -1 is indecision (body <= 50% of bar -2 body). Current bar is bearish and closes below bar -2 open.

entry_signals.timeframe_note: Entry signals assessed on the 15m chart. The 5m chart may be used for early entry refinement when the 15m structure is still forming.

stop_loss.placement: Set at the most recent swing high (for SHORT trades) or swing low (for LONG trades) at the time of entry.
stop_loss.minimum_distance_note: Stop loss must be a minimum of 150 DXY points (1500 pts / 0.1500 DXY) from the entry price. If the nearest swing high/low is closer than 150 pts, do not take the trade or extend to the next valid swing level.
stop_loss.rule: Stop is placed behind the structural swing level, not a fixed pip distance. The 150 pt minimum is a floor, not the target distance.

take_profit.primary_target: DXY Open Zone edge (zone_bottom for longs, zone_top for shorts).
take_profit.minimum_rr: 1:1 — Take Profit distance must be at least equal to the Stop Loss distance.
take_profit.partial_profits: At the primary target, close ½ to ⅔ of the position. Leave a runner if the HTF context supports continuation.
take_profit.runner_management: Check 4H and 1H bias. If initial trade direction aligns with 4H trend, consider leaving the remainder open. Reassess at target: close fully, reverse, or continue as swing.

risk_management.risk_per_trade: Maximum 1% of account equity per DXY trade.

news_filter.rule: Check economic calendar before every trade. Avoid entering during high-impact news events (NFP, FOMC, CPI, PMI, retail sales, JOLTS, durable goods).
news_filter.management: If already in a trade and high-impact news is imminent, consider exiting before the release or waiting until the market settles.

london_entry_window.rationale: Entry window runs 06:45–19:30 GMT daily including Monday. Covers pre-London open through NY afternoon close. Validated against backtesting — earliest winner in dataset entered at 06:45 GMT.
london_entry_window.pre_london_note: The 06:45 GMT start is the earliest permissible entry. Entries before 06:45 GMT are not taken.

japan_session.purpose: Defines the Open Zone and establishes directional bias. Zone pristine status monitored throughout.
japan_session.no_trade_rule: ABSOLUTE — No trade entries or signals during the Japan session (23:45–06:00 GMT). This session is observation-only. The entry window starts at 07:00 GMT, well after Japan session ends at 06:00 GMT, ensuring no overlap.

session_rules.zone_reset: Zone top, bottom, and pristine status reset at each new 23:45 GMT bar.

no_trade_conditions: Japan session active (23:45–06:00 GMT) — no entries, signals, or alerts. Observation only.
Price ranging tightly around the DXY Open Zone with no clear directional expansion.
Zone has been bridged repeatedly during Japan session — no clear bias.
Strong directional momentum during Japan session that may negate an attraction trade.
No impulsive bounce candle preceding the entry signal (reversal trades only).
AlgoPro Market Matrix divergence does not match trade direction (reversal trades only).
MTFTI 1H or 4H bias conflicts with trade direction — manual check required before acting on any reversal alert (reversal trades only).
High-impact news imminent during the trade window.
Less than 150 pts between entry and target (insufficient room for 1:1 RR).
No valid entry signal (engulfing, pin bar, or 3-bar reversal) — no signal, no trade.
workflow_checklist: SUNDAY: Review weekly fundamentals and high-impact news calendar.
DAILY (Weekly/Daily chart): Check 50/100/200 SMA trend. Mark S&D zones and Fibonacci confluence levels.
DAILY (4H chart): Confirm Daily bias alignment. Check MACD momentum and divergence.
23:45 GMT: Mark the DXY Open Zone on the 15m chart. Note whether Japan session opens above or below the prior candle close.
JAPAN SESSION: Monitor for zone pristine status. Note if any candle closes beyond the far zone edge.
PRE-LONDON: Assess Japan session momentum. Check MTFTI 1H and 4H bias.
LONDON SESSION: Wait for price to expand away from zone (min 150 pts). Identify trade type (Attraction or Reversal).
ATTRACTION (pristine zone): Confirm 150–500 pt distance. Look for entry signal. No HTF bias required.

REVERSAL (broken zone): Wait for impulsive bounce candle (>= 45 DXY pts, body >= 2x prior non-doji). Confirm AlgoPro Market Matrix divergence matches direction (automated). Manually check MTFTI table — 1H AND 4H bias must match direction. Then look for entry signal within 5 bars.
PRE-ENTRY: Check news calendar. Confirm SL placement at recent swing high/low (min 150 pts). Confirm 1:1 RR is achievable.
ENTRY: Place trade with defined SL and TP. Risk ≤ 1% account.
MANAGEMENT: At target, take partial profits (½ to ⅔). Reevaluate runner based on HTF context. If DXY zone is hit, manually exit all correlated FX pair trades.

trade_execution_on_correlated_pairs.description: Trades are executed on USD major FX pairs in correlation with the DXY direction. Begin with 2–3 pairs; experienced traders can trade all pairs simultaneously.
trade_execution_on_correlated_pairs.fx_pair_stop_loss: 25 pips default, adjusted for pair structure. Verify no structural level between entry and target that could prevent reaching the TP.
trade_execution_on_correlated_pairs.manual_exit_rule: If DXY level is hit, manually close ALL correlated FX pair trades even if individual pair targets have not been reached.

distance_units.explanation: Distance is measured in 'pts' where 1 pt = 0.0001 DXY (equivalent to 1 pip on a 4-decimal FX pair).

examples.150_dxy_points: = 1500 pts in the indicator = 0.1500 DXY price move
examples.500_dxy_points: = 5000 pts in the indicator = 0.5000 DXY price move

alerts.attraction_long: Zone PRISTINE, Japan bearish, price 150–500 pts below zone_bottom, bullish entry signal in session. Go LONG USD pairs.
alerts.attraction_short: Zone PRISTINE, Japan bullish, price 150–500 pts above zone_top, bearish entry signal in session. Go SHORT USD pairs.
alerts.reversal_long: Zone BROKEN, impulsive bullish bounce (>= 45 DXY pts, >= 2x prev body), AlgoPro bullish divergence — then manually confirm MTFTI 1H+4H bullish before acting. Bullish entry signal. Go LONG USD pairs.
alerts.reversal_short: Zone BROKEN, impulsive bearish bounce (>= 45 DXY pts, >= 2x prev body), AlgoPro bearish divergence — then manually confirm MTFTI 1H+4H bearish before acting. Bearish entry signal. Go SHORT USD pairs.
alerts.any_setup: Catch-all alert for any of the four conditions. Check chart for zone status and direction.

visual_outputs.open_zone_box: Shaded band between zone_top and zone_bottom. Orange = pristine, grey = broken.
visual_outputs.attraction_bands: Lines at zone_top ± attr_max_pts and ± attr_min_pts showing the valid attraction entry distance window.
visual_outputs.london_session_background: Light blue background shading during the London entry window.

labels.attraction_long: Green label below bar — signal name, pts from zone, target price.
labels.attraction_short: Red label above bar — signal name, pts from zone, target price.
labels.reversal_long: Teal label below bar — signal name, zone bottom level.
labels.reversal_short: Purple label above bar — signal name, zone top level.

historical_test.label: Historical Backtest — May–Dec 2024
historical_test.zone_data: 15-minute OHLCV bars collected via TradingView replay, chunked May–Dec 2024
historical_test.match_rate_with_zone_data: 76% (26 of 34 trades where zone data was available)
historical_test.manual_check_note: 16 trades have missing 23:45 bar data. Require manual chart review — sideways London open = no trade.

performance_at_1000_risk_per_trade.win_rate_note: 100% — dataset is confirmed winners only. Real-world win rate will be lower.

forward_test.label: Forward Test — Feb 1 – Mar 31, 2026
forward_test.method: Pine Script indicator simulation — process_orders_on_close semantics
forward_test.market_context: DXY in strong downtrend (~108 Jan → ~98 Apr 2026)

performance_at_1000_risk_per_trade.note: All raw unfiltered signals. Trending market challenged zone reversal strategy.
